name: Clarent Sword
description: Clarent was a sword of peace, meant for knighting ceremonies and other peaceful occasions. It belonged to King Arthur and was kept in his armory. It is described as a great, bright sword and was often used for ceremonial purposes, symbolizing Arthur's authority and commitment to justice. However, the sword's peaceful nature was tragically subverted when Mordred stole it from Arthur's armory and used it to wound the king mortally at the Battle of Camlann. This act represents a betrayal of Arthur's ideals and the corruption of a symbol of peace into an instrument of destruction. Clarent's theft and misuse are often seen as a contributing factor to the downfall of Camelot, highlighting the fragility of peace and the devastating consequences of treachery. The sword's beauty and ceremonial purpose stand in stark contrast to the brutal reality of its use in Arthur's demise, making it a poignant symbol of the Arthurian legend's themes of honor, betrayal, and the fall of a kingdom. It's often described as being inlaid with precious stones and having a hilt of gold, signifying its importance and value.
subtitle: A sword of peace turned to betrayal.

keyMyths.title: Mordred's Treachery
keyMyths.description: Mordred stole Clarent from Arthur's armory and used it to fatally wound the king at the Battle of Camlann, betraying Arthur's trust.

keyMyths.title: Sword of Peace
keyMyths.description: Clarent was intended as a sword of peace, used for knighting ceremonies and other peaceful occasions, representing Arthur's commitment to justice.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A compilation of Arthurian legends, including the story of Clarent's theft and misuse.
sources.text: A compilation of Arthurian legends, including the story of Clarent's theft and misuse.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
sources.description: An earlier account of Arthurian legends, although details about Clarent may vary.
